---
description: "Use when modifying frontend UI, pages, components, styling, routing, forms, or client-side state. Covers UX consistency, responsiveness, states, and accessibility."
name: "Frontend Guidance"
---

# Frontend Guidance

- Preserve the existing design system, spacing rhythm, and interaction patterns before introducing new UI patterns.
- Cover empty, loading, error, and success states for user-facing flows.
- Keep keyboard access, focus order, labels, and contrast in scope for any interactive change.
- Prefer small component-level changes over broad page rewrites unless the task explicitly requires larger layout work.
- When behavior changes, add or update tests that cover the user-visible outcome rather than implementation details.
